evals.question_short: COMMIT through physical streaming with remote_apply

assertions.text: Identifies the four processes: committing backend, walsender (primary), walreceiver (standby), startup process (standby)
Notes walreceiver is NOT linked against libpq directly — uses dynamically loaded libpqwalreceiver/
Mentions MAX_SEND_SIZE = 16 * XLOG_BLCKSZ = 128 KB batch size
Explains that with remote_apply the backend waits on SyncRepQueue keyed by commit LSN and walsender wakes it on feedback
Distinguishes the three feedback levels (write_lsn / flush_lsn / apply_lsn) matched to remote_write / on / remote_apply
Notes that a standby must already be caught up to count toward the sync policy (configuration alone insufficient)
Notes the shutdown-order subtlety: walsenders wait until AFTER the shutdown checkpoint, opposite of normal backends
Cites at least one source file:line (e.g. syncrep.c, walsender.c, README) for a specific claim
Names decode.c → reorderbuffer.c → output plugin → walsender/SRF as the three-stage pipeline
Explains that WAL is in record order not transaction order, so reorder buffer groups by xid and emits at commit
Notes that decode.c also calls ReorderBufferProcessXid for non-data records to keep subxact trees consistent
Names the two memory contexts used by reorder buffer: SlabContext (fixed-size) and GenerationContext (variable-length)
States that on memory pressure the LARGEST in-progress transaction is spilled (via max-heap keyed by size)
Mentions logical_decoding_work_mem as the per-reorderbuffer memory limit GUC
Explains snapbuild.c builds historical MVCC catalog snapshots at the LSN of the change
Names the output_plugin.h callback contract including _PG_output_plugin_init and OutputPluginCallbacks
Mentions test_decoding (contrib) and pgoutput as canonical / built-in output plugins
Notes toast chunks are reassembled inline within a toplevel txn
States that v17 introduced slot sync to let logical subscriptions survive failover without resnapshotting
Identifies the failover=true flag on the slot and sync_replication_slots GUC on the standby
Names the slot-sync worker in logical/slotsync.c as the puller on the standby
Lists all THREE sync-ready preconditions: standby WAL past remote confirmed_flush_lsn; catalog xmin not behind; consistent snapshot buildable at restart_lsn before confirmed_flush_lsn
Notes that mirror slots are RS_TEMPORARY until sync-ready, then flip to RS_PERSISTENT with synced=true
Explains synchronized_standby_slots gates the logical slot's confirmed_flush from advancing past lagging physical standbys
Names the wakeup chain: WalSndWaitForWal → NeedToWaitForStandbys/NeedToWaitForWal → PhysicalWakeupLogicalWalSnd → wal_confirm_rcv_cv condition variable
Notes synced slots are treated as inactive for idle-timeout purposes
Cites slotsync.c:11-35 or walsender.c:1801-1886 or slot.c lines for a specific claim
